ead.archdesc:
======= RMA01466Howard S. Liddell papers14-23-1466Compiled by D.B., G.S.Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

socsc lifesThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:07:30 UTC.ENG12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Howard S. Liddell papers
Liddell, H. S. (Howard Scott), 1895-
14-23-1466
15 cubic feet. 15 cubic feet. 1 tape recording.,
1920-1967. Correspondence, lecture and research notes, course materials, financial and subject files, manuscripts, printed materials, photographs, diagrams, glass slides and negatives, movie film, tape recording. Personal and professional papers and materials of Cornell University professor of psychobiology, founder of the Behavior Farm Laboratory. Collection material in English 2073582
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
D.B., G.S.
Date completed:
Sept. 1984
Date modified:
RMC Staff, September 2013
Biographical / Historical
Biographic chronology
CHRONOLOGY
1917
Graduated Univ. of Michigan
1918
MA
1923
Cornell Univ. Ph.D.
1919
Cornell Univ. Instructor
1926
Cornell Univ. Assistant Prof.
1930
Cornell Univ. Chairman of Dept. of Physiology in the Medical College
1939
Cornell Univ. Prof. of Psychology
1947
Cornell Univ. Prof. of Psychobiology
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Personal papers include school notebooks, 1920's, and miscellany. Professional papers include correspondence, acknowledgements, recommendations for students, Department of Psychology and Behavior Farm Laboratory correspondence; conference and lecture notes, manuscripts, programs, and memos, 1952-1963; student notes, rollbooks, and course materials; research notebooks and files, ca. 1930-1959; Department of Psychology financial files, 1926-1963, and subject files on topics including the Behavior Farm, equipment, and procedures; manuscripts and printed materials include reprints by Liddell and other authors; also visual materials such as photograph albums, diagrams, glass slides and negatives, and movie film dealing with pig, sheep, dog, and goat experiments at the Behavior Farm. Correspondents include the American Russian Institute, American Society for Research in Psychosomatic Problems, Association for Advancement of Psychoanalysis, W. Horsley Gantt, A. U. Moore, Leon J. Saul, J. Paul Scott, graduate students, publishers, and other scientists and psychologists.
SERIES LIST
Photographs
Diagrams
Glass Slides and Negatives
Movie Films
Cite As:
Howard S. Liddell Papers, #14-23-1466. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Motion pictures (visual works). Psychology -- Study and teaching. Psychology -- Research. Scott, John Paul, 1909-2000. Gantt, W. Horsley (William Horsley), 1892-1980. Saul, Leon J. (Leon Joseph), 1901-1983. Moore, A. U. Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Students. American Russian Institute American Society for Research in Psychosomatic Problems Association for the Advancement of Psychoanalysis
